features.name: Gifted Performer
features.description:
You can play three different types of songs, once each per long rest; describe how you perform for others to gain the listed benefit:

- **Relaxing Song:** You and all allies within Close range clear a Hit Point.
- **Epic Song:** Make a target within Close range temporarily Vulnerable.
- **Heartbreaking Song:** You and all allies within Close range gain a Hope.

features.name: Maestro
features.description: Your rallying songs steel the courage of those who listen. When you give a Rally Die to an ally, they can gain a Hope or clear a Stress.

features.name: Virtuoso
features.description: Virtuoso

features.name: Rousing Speech
features.description: Once per long rest, you can give a heartfelt, inspiring speech. All allies within Far range clear 2 Stress.

features.name: Heart of a Poet
features.description: After you make an action roll to impress, persuade, or offend someone, you can spend a Hope to add a d4 to the roll.

features.name: Eloquent
features.description:
Your moving words boost morale. Once per session, when you encourage an ally, you can do one of the following:

- Allow them to find a mundane object or tool they need.
- Help an Ally without spending Hope.
- Give them an additional downtime move during their next rest.

features.name: Epic Poetry
features.description: Epic Poetry

features.name: Elemental Incarnation
features.description:
Mark a Stress to Channel one of the following elements until you take Severe damage or until your next rest:

- **Fire:** When an adversary within Melee range deals damage to you, they take 1d10 magic damage.
- **Earth:** Gain a bonus to your damage thresholds equal to your Proficiency.
- **Water:** When you deal damage to an adversary within Melee range, all other adversaries within Very Close range must mark a Stress.
- **Air:** You can hover, gaining advantage on Agility Rolls.

features.name: Elemental Aura
features.description:
Once per rest while Channeling, you can assume an aura matching your element. The aura affects targets within Close range until your Channeling ends.

- **Fire:** When an adversary marks 1 or more Hit Points, they must also mark a Stress.
- **Earth:** Your allies gain a +1 bonus to Strength.
- **Water:** When an adversary deals damage to you, you can mark a Stress to move them anywhere within Very Close range of where they are.
- **Air:** When you or an ally takes damage from an attack beyond Melee range, reduce the damage by 1d8.

features.name: Elemental Dominion
features.description: Elemental Dominion

features.name: Clarity of Nature
features.description: Once per long rest, you can create a space of natural serenity within Close range. When you spend a few minutes resting within the space, clear Stress equal to your Instinct, distributed as you choose between you and your allies.

features.name: Regeneration
features.description: Touch a creature and spend 3 Hope. That creature clears 1d4 Hit Points.

features.name: Regenerative Reach
features.description: You can target creatures within Very Close range with your "Regeneration" feature.

features.name: Warden’s Protection
features.description: Once per long rest, spend 2 Hope to clear 2 Hit Points on 1d4 allies within Close range.

features.name: Defender
features.description: Defender

features.name: Unwavering
features.description: Gain a permanent +1 bonus to your damage thresholds.

features.name: Iron Will
features.description: When you take physical damage, you can mark an additional Armor Slot to reduce the severity.

features.name: Unrelenting
features.description: Gain a permanent +2 bonus to your damage thresholds.

features.name: Partners-in-Arms
features.description: When an ally within Very Close range takes damage, you can mark an Armor Slot to reduce the severity by one threshold.

features.name: Undaunted
features.description: Undaunted

features.name: Loyal Protector
features.description: When an ally within Close range has 2 or fewer Hit Points and would take damage, you can mark a Stress to sprint to their side and take the damage instead.

features.name: At Ease
features.description: Gain an additional Stress slot.

features.name: Revenge
features.description: When an adversary within Melee range succeeds on an attack against you, you can mark 2 Stress to force the attacker to mark a Hit Point.

features.name: Act of Reprisal
features.description: When an adversary damages an ally within Melee range, you gain a +1 bonus to your Proficiency for the next successful attack you make against that adversary.

features.name: Nemesis
features.description: Nemesis

features.name: Companion
features.description:
You have an animal companion of your choice (at the GM's discretion). They stay by your side unless you tell them otherwise.

Take the Ranger Companion sheet. When you level up your character, choose a level-up option for your companion from this sheet as well.

features.name: Expert Training
features.description: Choose an additional level-up option for your companion.

features.name: Battle-Bonded
features.description: When an adversary attacks you while they're within your companion's Melee range, you gain a +2 bonus to your Evasion against the attack.

features.name: Advanced Training
features.description: Advanced Training

features.name: Loyal Friend
features.description: Once per long rest, when the damage from an attack would mark your companion's last Stress or your last Hit Point and you're within Close range of each other, you or your companion can rush to the other's side and take that damage instead.

features.name: Ruthless Predator
features.description: When you make a damage roll, you can mark a Stress to gain a +1 bonus to your Proficiency. Additionally, when you deal Severe damage to an adversary, they must mark a Stress.

features.name: Path Forward
features.description: When you're traveling to a place you've previously visited or you carry an object that has been at the location before, you can identify the shortest, most direct path to your destination.

features.name: Elusive Predator
features.description: When your Focus makes an attack against you, you gain a +2 bonus to your Evasion against the attack.

features.name: Apex Predator
features.description: Apex Predator

features.name: Shadow Stepper
features.description: You can move from shadow to shadow. When you move into an area of darkness or a shadow cast by another creature or object, you can **mark a Stress** to disappear from where you are and reappear inside another shadow within Far range. When you reappear, you are Cloaked.

features.name: Dark Cloud
features.description: Make a Spellcast Roll (15). On a success, create a temporary dark cloud that covers any area within Close range. Anyone in this cloud can't see outside of it, and anyone outside of it can't see in. You're considered Cloaked from any adversary for whom the cloud blocks line of sight.

features.name: Adrenaline
features.description: While you're Vulnerable, add your level to your damage rolls.

features.name: Fleeting Shadow
features.description: Fleeting Shadow

features.name: Vanishing Act
features.description: Mark a Stress to become Cloaked at any time. When Cloaked from this feature, you automatically clear the Restrained condition if you have it. You remain Cloaked in this way until you roll with Fear or until your next rest.

features.name: Well-Connected
features.description:
When you arrive in a prominent town or environment, you know somebody who calls this place home. Give them a name, note how you think they could be useful, and choose one fact from the following list:

- They owe me a favor, but they'll be hard to find.
- They're going to ask for something in exchange.
- They're always in a great deal of trouble.
- We used to be together. It's a long story.
- We didn't part on great terms.

features.name: Contacts Everywhere
features.description:
Once per session, you can briefly call on a shady contact. Choose one of the following benefits and describe what brought them here to help you in this moment:

- They provide 1 handful of gold, a unique tool, or a mundane object that the situation requires.
- On your next action roll, their help provides a +3 bonus to the result of your Hope or Fear Die.
- The next time you deal damage, they snipe from the shadows, adding 2d8 to your damage roll.

features.name: Reliable Backup
features.description: Reliable Backup

features.name: Spirit Weapon
features.description: When you have an equipped weapon with a range of Melee or Very Close, it can fly from your hand to attack an adversary within Close range and then return to you. You can mark a Stress to target an additional adversary within range with the same attack roll.

features.name: Sparing Touch
features.description: Once per long rest, touch a creature and clear 2 Hit Points or 2 Stress from them.

features.name: Devout
features.description: When you roll your Prayer Dice, you can roll an additional die and discard the lowest result. Additionally, you can use your "Sparing Touch" feature twice instead of once per long rest.

features.name: Sacred Resonance
features.description: Sacred Resonance

features.name: Wings of Light
features.description:
You can fly. While flying, you can do the following:

- Mark a Stress to pick up and carry another willing creature approximately your size or smaller.
- Spend a Hope to deal an extra 1d8 damage on a successful attack.

features.name: Ethereal Visage
features.description: Your supernatural visage strikes awe and fear. While flying, you have advantage on Presence Rolls. When you succeed with Hope on a Presence Roll, you can remove a Fear from the GM's Fear pool instead of gaining Hope.

features.name: Ascendant
features.description: Ascendant

features.name: Power of the Gods
features.description: While flying, you deal an extra 1d12 damage instead of 1d8 from your "Wings of Light" feature.

features.name: Elementalist
features.description:
Choose one of the following elements at character creation: air, earth, fire, lightning, water.

You can shape this element into harmless effects. Additionally, spend a Hope and describe how your control over this element helps an action roll you're about to make, then either gain a +2 bonus to the roll or a +3 bonus to the roll's damage.

features.name: Natural Evasion
features.description: You can call forth your element to protect you from harm. When an attack roll against you succeeds, you can mark a Stress and describe how you use your element to defend you. When you do, roll a d6 and add its result to your Evasion against the attack.

features.name: Transcendence
features.description: Transcendence

features.name: Manipulate Magic
features.description:
Your primal origin allows you to modify the essence of magic itself. After you cast a spell or make an attack using a weapon that deals magic damage, you can mark a Stress to do one of the following:

- Extend the spell or attack's reach by one range
- Gain a +2 bonus to the action roll's result
- Double a damage die of your choice
- Hit an additional target within range

features.name: Enchanted Aid
features.description: You can enhance the magic of others with your essence. When you Help an Ally with a Spellcast Roll, you can roll a d8 as your advantage die. Once per long rest, after an ally has made a Spellcast Roll with your help, you can swap the results of their Duality Dice.

features.name: Arcane Charge
features.description: Arcane Charge

features.name: Courage
features.description: When you fail a roll with Fear, you gain a Hope.

features.name: Battle Ritual
features.description: Once per long rest, before you attempt something incredibly dangerous or face off against a foe who clearly outmatches you, describe what ritual you perform or preparations you make. When you do, clear 2 Stress and gain 2 Hope.

features.name: Rise to the Challenge
features.description: You are vigilant in the face of mounting danger. While you have 2 or fewer Hit Points unmarked, you can roll a d20 as your Hope Die.

features.name: Camaraderie
features.description: Camaraderie

features.name: Slayer
features.description: You gain a pool of dice called Slayer Dice. On a roll with Hope, you can place a d6 on this card instead of gaining a Hope, adding the die to the pool. You can store a number of Slayer Dice equal to your Proficiency. When you make an attack roll or damage roll, you can spend any number of these Slayer Dice, rolling them and adding their result to the roll. At the end of each session, clear any unspent Slayer Dice on this card and gain a Hope per die cleared.

features.name: Weapon Specialist
features.description: You can wield multiple weapons with dangerous ease. When you succeed on an attack, you can spend a Hope to add one of the damage dice from your secondary weapon to the damage roll. Additionally, once per long rest when you roll your Slayer Dice, reroll any 1s.

features.name: Martial Preparation
features.description: Martial Preparation

features.name: Prepared
features.description: Take an additional domain card of your level or lower from a domain you have access to.

features.name: Adept
features.description: When you Utilize an Experience, you can mark a Stress instead of spending a Hope. If you do, double your Experience modifier for that roll.

features.name: Accomplished
features.description: Take an additional domain card of your level or lower from a domain you have access to.

features.name: Perfect Recall
features.description: Once per rest, when you recall a domain card in your vault, you can reduce its Recall Cost by 1.

features.name: Brilliant
features.description: Brilliant

features.name: Honed Expertise
features.description: When you use an Experience, roll a d6. On a result of 5 or higher, you can use it without spending Hope.

features.name: Battlemage
features.description: You've focused your studies on becoming an unconquerable force on the battlefield. Gain an additional Hit Point slot.

features.name: Face Your Fear
features.description: When you succeed with Fear on an attack roll, you deal an extra 1d10 magic damage.

features.name: Conjure Shield
features.description: You can maintain a protective barrier of magic. While you have at least 2 Hope, you add your Proficiency to your Evasion.

features.name: Fueled by Fear
features.description: The extra magic damage from your "Face Your Fear" feature increases to 2d10.

features.name: Thrive in Chaos
features.description: Thrive in Chaos

features.name: Have No Fear
features.description: The extra magic damage from your "Face Your Fear" feature increases to 3d10.
